sources_used.title: Humanity's Last Exam
sources_used.publisher: Center for AI Safety / Scale AI (agi.safe.ai)

sources_used.title: OpenAI /index/osworld/ (scrape result)
sources_used.notes: Scrape returned 404 content; cannot extract OSWorld benchmark details from this URL in the captured artifact.

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 features a 1M token context window in beta.
claims.quote_snippet: "Opus 4.6 features a 1M token context window in beta"

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 improves on its predecessor’s coding skills: plans more carefully, sustains agentic tasks for longer, operates more reliably in larger codebases, and has better code review and debugging skills.
claims.quote_snippet: "It plans more carefully, sustains agentic tasks for longer, can operate more reliably in larger codebases, and has better code review and debugging skills to catch its own mistakes."
claims.methodology_notes: Not a benchmark claim; qualitative vendor description.

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 achieves the highest score on Terminal-Bench 2.0.
claims.quote_snippet: "it achieves the highest score on the agentic coding evaluation Terminal-Bench 2.0"
claims.methodology_notes: Terminal-Bench 2.0 is an agentic coding evaluation focused on terminal/system tasks (per Anthropic). Independent benchmark maintainer notes that TB2.0 is 'harder' and 'better verified' than TB1.0 with substantial manual + LM-assisted verification of tasks (tbench.ai).

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 leads all other frontier models on Humanity’s Last Exam.
claims.quote_snippet: "[it] leads all other frontier models on Humanity’s Last Exam"
claims.methodology_notes: HLE is described as a complex multidisciplinary reasoning test by Anthropic; the HLE site notes the dataset was finalized with 2,500 questions and published in Nature (agi.safe.ai). Specific leaderboard evidence was not captured in the scraped HLE page excerpt.

claims.claim_text: On GDPval-AA, Opus 4.6 outperforms the next-best model (OpenAI’s GPT-5.2) by around 144 Elo points.
claims.quote_snippet: "Opus 4.6 outperforms the industry’s next-best model (OpenAI’s GPT-5.2) by around 144 Elo points"
claims.methodology_notes: GDPval-AA is an Artificial Analysis evaluation framework for OpenAI’s GDPval dataset; models get shell + web browsing in an agentic loop via Stirrup; ELO derived from blind pairwise comparisons (Artificial Analysis). The scraped GDPval-AA page excerpt did not include GPT-5.2 rows to validate the exact 144 Elo gap.

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 performs better than any other model on BrowseComp.
claims.quote_snippet: "Opus 4.6 also performs better than any other model on BrowseComp"
claims.methodology_notes: BrowseComp is described (by Anthropic) as measuring ability to locate hard-to-find information online. No independent BrowseComp leaderboard/methodology was captured in the current evidence set.

claims.claim_text: Opus 4.6 shows an overall safety profile as good as, or better than, any other frontier model in the industry, with low rates of misaligned behavior across safety evaluations.
claims.quote_snippet: "Opus 4.6 also shows an overall safety profile as good as, or better than, any other frontier model in the industry, with low rates of misaligned behavior across safety evaluations."
claims.methodology_notes: Vendor references an 'extensive system card' for details; system card not scraped in this task run, so methods cannot be summarized here.

claims.claim_text: Claude Opus 4.6 pricing remains the same at $5/$25 per million tokens.
claims.quote_snippet: "Pricing remains the same at $5/$25 per million tokens"

claims.claim_text: GPT-5.3-Codex is the most capable agentic coding model to date.
claims.quote_snippet: "GPT‑5.3‑Codex, the most capable agentic coding model to date"
claims.methodology_notes: Positioning claim; benchmark evidence described separately in other claims.
No measurement definition (e.g., tokens/sec, end-to-end latency) captured in excerpt.

claims.claim_text: GPT-5.3-Codex is OpenAI’s first model that was instrumental in creating itself.
claims.quote_snippet: "GPT‑5.3‑Codex is our first model that was instrumental in creating itself."
claims.methodology_notes: Anecdotal internal process claim; not benchmarked.

claims.claim_text: GPT-5.3-Codex sets a new industry high on SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench, and shows strong performance on OSWorld and GDPval.
claims.quote_snippet: "GPT‑5.3‑Codex sets a new industry high on SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench, and shows strong performance on OSWorld and GDPval"
claims.methodology_notes: OpenAI frames these as four benchmarks measuring coding/agentic/real-world capabilities. OSWorld details were not retrievable from the scraped openai.com/index/osworld URL (404). GDPval-AA methodology captured from Artificial Analysis; SWE-Bench Pro methodology details not captured in current independent sources.

claims.claim_text: GPT-5.3-Codex far exceeds prior state-of-the-art on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and (in the post figure) is shown at 77.3% accuracy vs 64.0% for GPT-5.2-Codex and 62.2% for GPT-5.2.
claims.methodology_notes: Terminal-Bench 2.0 described by tbench.ai as 'harder' and 'better verified' than 1.0, with substantial manual and LM-assisted verification per task. OpenAI provides accuracy numbers in a chart; independent reproduction/leaderboard cross-check not captured here.

claims.claim_text: Terminal-Bench 2.0 is a harder, better verified version of Terminal-Bench, with substantial manual and LM-assisted verification per task.
claims.quote_snippet: "Terminal-Bench 2.0 is a harder, better verified version Terminal-Bench. We conducted substantial manual and LM-assisted verification of the dataset"
claims.methodology_notes: Benchmark maintainer statement about dataset verification and quality.

claims.claim_text: Humanity’s Last Exam is noted as finalized with 2,500 questions and published in Nature.
claims.quote_snippet: "HLE has been finalized with 2,500 questions"; "HLE is published on Nature"
claims.methodology_notes: Benchmark maintainer/news notes; does not itself validate any particular model's ranking.

claims.claim_text: GDPval-AA is Artificial Analysis’ evaluation framework for OpenAI’s GDPval dataset; models get shell access and web browsing in an agentic loop via Stirrup; ELO is from blind pairwise comparisons.
claims.quote_snippet: "Models are given shell access and web browsing capabilities in an agentic loop via Stirrup"; "ELO ratings derived from blind pairwise comparisons"

claims.claim_text: Artificial Analysis highlights top GDPval-AA scores: Claude Sonnet 4.6 at 1633 and Claude Opus 4.6 at 1606 (with another Opus 4.6 configuration at 1579).
claims.quote_snippet: "Claude Sonnet 4.6 ... 1633 ... followed by Claude Opus 4.6 ... 1606 ... and Claude Opus 4.6 ... 1579"
claims.methodology_notes: These are highlighted scores on the GDPval-AA page; configuration details in the highlight include reasoning/effort settings.

placeholders.notes: Cells intentionally left empty when claims were not captured in current evidence set (e.g., independent SWE-Bench Pro leaderboard, OSWorld details, BrowseComp methodology).

known_gaps: No independent leaderboard capture for Terminal-Bench 2.0 results to reconcile Anthropic 'highest score' vs OpenAI 'new industry high'/77.3% claims.
OSWorld URL scrape returned 404; no independent OSWorld benchmark page captured.
No independent corroboration captured for 1M token context window, BrowseComp ranking, or safety-profile claims (system card not included).
GDPval-AA page excerpt captured methodology and top highlighted Claude scores, but did not include GPT-5.2 row needed to verify the exact '144 Elo' gap claim.
